project: Hal Cloud Daytona Sandbox Orchestration v1
description: Build v1 cloud orchestration for hal workloads in Daytona sandboxes with terminal-first lifecycle commands, durable run state, Postgres and Turso storage parity, auth profile lifecycle management, idempotent GitHub side effects, and strict secret redaction.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create runs table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want durable run records so orchestration can recover after restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a `runs` table with columns `id`, `repo`, `base_branch`, `engine`, `auth_profile_id`, `scope_ref`, `status`, `attempt_count`, `max_attempts`, `deadline_at`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`.
Create an index on `(status, created_at)` for queue reads.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create attempts table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want attempt-level state so heartbeat and retry behavior is explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an `attempts` table with columns `id`, `run_id`, `attempt_number`, `worker_id`, `sandbox_id`, `status`, `started_at`, `heartbeat_at`, `lease_expires_at`, `ended_at`, `error_code`, and `error_message`.
Enforce at most one active attempt per `run_id`.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create events table
userStories.description: As an operator, I want durable run timelines so status and logs commands read authoritative history.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an `events` table with columns `id`, `run_id`, `attempt_id`, `event_type`, `payload_json`, `redacted`, and `created_at`.
Ensure submit, worker, and reconciler paths append new events only and do not update or delete existing event rows.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create idempotency table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want persisted idempotency keys so retries cannot duplicate external side effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an `idempotency_keys` table with columns `key`, `run_id`, `side_effect_type`, `result_ref`, and `created_at`.
Return a deterministic `duplicate_key` domain error when inserting a duplicate key.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profiles table
userStories.description: As an operator, I want reusable auth profiles so credentials are linked once and reused by workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an `auth_profiles` table with columns `id`, `owner_id`, `provider`, `mode`, `secret_ref`, `status`, `max_concurrent_runs`, `runtime_metadata_json`, `last_validated_at`, `expires_at`, `last_error_code`, `version`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profile locks table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want lease-based locks so shared subscription sessions are concurrency-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an `auth_profile_locks` table with columns `auth_profile_id`, `run_id`, `worker_id`, `acquired_at`, `heartbeat_at`, `lease_expires_at`, and `released_at`.
Add a constraint preventing duplicate active lock rows for the same `(auth_profile_id, run_id)`.

userStories.title: [Backend] Define Store interface and domain errors
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a single persistence contract so orchestration logic is adapter-agnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: Define `Store` methods for enqueue and claim transitions, attempt heartbeats, event persistence, idempotency operations, auth profile read and update, and lock acquire, renew, and release.
Compile the service layer without adapter-specific imports.

userStories.title: [Backend] Add adapter contract test suite skeleton
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want adapter-neutral tests so correctness rules are defined once.
acceptanceCriteria: Add contract test cases for enqueue, atomic claim, one-active-attempt invariant, heartbeat renew, and transition guards.
Add a parallel claim test that asserts one winner per run.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Postgres enqueue and claim operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Postgres run-claim primitives so workers can safely pick work.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Postgres methods for run enqueue and atomic claim.
Ensure claim SQL is concurrency-safe under parallel workers and allows only one winner per run.
Add focused tests for enqueue and one-winner claim behavior.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Postgres auth lock lease operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Postgres lock primitives so auth profile concurrency is enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Postgres methods for auth lock acquire, renew, and release.
Enforce lease-expiry semantics on renew operations.
Add focused tests for lock acquisition conflict and stale-lock reclaim paths.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Turso enqueue and claim operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Turso run-claim primitives so workers can safely pick work.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Turso methods for run enqueue and atomic claim.
Match one-winner claim semantics under contention.
Add focused tests for enqueue and one-winner claim behavior.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Turso auth lock lease operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Turso lock primitives so auth profile concurrency is enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Turso methods for auth lock acquire, renew, and release.
Enforce lease-expiry semantics on renew operations.
Add focused tests for lock acquisition conflict and stale-lock reclaim paths.

userStories.title: [Backend] Run adapter parity benchmark harness
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want comparable adapter metrics so the default database choice is evidence-based.
acceptanceCriteria: Execute benchmark scenarios `claim_contention` and `auth_lock_contention` for both adapters.
Emit JSON results with exactly these keys: `scenario`, `adapter`, `runs`, `errors`, `claim_p95_ms`, `heartbeat_p95_ms`, `duplicate_claims`, and `lock_overcommit_violations`.
Generate a report artifact for each adapter run.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement submit validation and enqueue
userStories.description: As an operator, I want invalid runs rejected before queueing.
acceptanceCriteria: Reject profiles not in `linked` status and reject engine-provider mismatches.
Enforce provider policy allow and deny rules before creating a queued run.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement atomic claim and lock acquisition transaction
userStories.description: As a worker, I want ownership acquisition to be all-or-nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: In one transaction, transition one eligible run to `claimed` and create the attempt row.
In the same transaction, acquire the auth lock lease.
If lock acquisition fails, roll back the run claim and attempt creation.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement heartbeat renewals
userStories.description: As a worker, I want lease renewals so healthy attempts are not reclaimed.
acceptanceCriteria: Heartbeat updates lock `heartbeat_at` and extends lock `lease_expires_at`.
On lease loss, emit terminal `lease_lost` event and stop the worker.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement stale-attempt reconciler
userStories.description: As an operator, I want stale attempts detected and closed automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Reconciler scans active attempts where `lease_expires_at < now`.
Reconciler marks each stale attempt terminal with `error_code=stale_attempt`.
Reconciler releases stale auth lock leases.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement retry scheduler with backoff
userStories.description: As an operator, I want deterministic retry transitions for retryable failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Support configurable `max_attempts` and exponential backoff in retry policy.
Transition retry-eligible runs through `failed -> retrying -> queued`.
Keep runs beyond `max_attempts` in terminal `failed` state.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement sandbox provisioning
userStories.description: As a worker, I want deterministic sandbox creation so each attempt has isolated runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Provision a Daytona sandbox from configured template or image.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement sandbox teardown workflow
userStories.description: As a worker, I want teardown to always run on terminal paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Execute teardown on both success and failure terminal paths.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement bootstrap setup steps
userStories.description: As a worker, I want deterministic environment setup before Hal execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Before clone, verify `git`, `hal`, and provider CLI binaries (`codex`, `claude`, or `pi`) are available.
Clone or fetch the repository and check out the target branch.
Run `hal init` only when `.hal/` is missing.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Hal execution runner
userStories.description: As a worker, I want a controlled execution step so run mode is explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist execution start and finish events including selected mode.
Capture command exit status for classifier input.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement failure classifier
userStories.description: As an operator, I want consistent failure codes for retry decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Map provider, preflight, bootstrap, and runtime failures to classifier codes.
Drive retry eligibility from classifier code mapping only.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement hard-timeout enforcement
userStories.description: As an operator, I want overdue runs terminated automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Set `deadline_at` during submit from defaults or policy.
Reconciler marks runs past deadline as `failed` with `error_code=run_timeout`.
Emit a `run_timeout` event on timeout transition.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement auth materialization with least-privilege permissions
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want worker credential materialization to be secure by default.
acceptanceCriteria: Materialize auth artifacts from `secret_ref` only at runtime.
Create auth directories with mode `0700` and files with mode `0600`.
Remove materialized auth files during teardown.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement provider preflight and compatibility checks
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want headless validation before Hal execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Run provider-specific non-interactive preflight command before execution.
Validate runtime metadata compatibility for OS, architecture, and CLI major/minor versions.
Fail with `auth_profile_incompatible` when metadata validation fails.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement credential writeback with version checks
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want refreshed credentials persisted safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect changed auth artifacts and write back encrypted secrets through Auth Broker.
On successful writeback, update `last_validated_at` and `expires_at`, and clear `last_error_code`.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement deterministic redaction rule engine
userStories.description: As an operator, I want deterministic masking so secret handling is testable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement explicit redaction rules for bearer tokens, GitHub PATs, device codes, session cookies, and API keys.
Provide fixture-based unit tests for each rule with expected masked outputs.
Use exact mask token `[REDACTED]` for all redactions.

userStories.title: [Backend] Integrate redaction into persistence and streaming pipelines
userStories.description: As an operator, I want every log path redacted before visibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply redaction before writing bootstrap, preflight, runtime, and error events.
Apply the same redaction before streaming logs to CLI clients.
Add integration tests proving raw fixture secrets do not appear in DB rows or CLI stream output.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement idempotent PR create side effect
userStories.description: As an operator, I want retries to reuse PR creation results.
acceptanceCriteria: Use a deterministic idempotency key for PR creation.
When key exists, return stored PR reference without issuing an external create call.
Verify in retry tests that exactly one external PR create call occurs.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement idempotent PR update and comment side effects
userStories.description: As an operator, I want retries to avoid duplicate PR follow-up actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Use deterministic idempotency keys for PR update and comment actions.
When key exists, return stored result without duplicate external calls.
Verify in retry tests that no duplicate comment or update side effects occur.

userStories.title: [Backend] Enforce cancellation propagation and claim exclusion
userStories.description: As an operator, I want cancellation intent enforced by workers and scheduler logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker loop checks cancel intent each heartbeat interval and stops active execution when cancel is set.
Claim query excludes runs already in terminal `canceled` state.
Retry scheduler skips runs marked as canceled.

userStories.title: [Backend] Enforce revoked-profile renewal denial
userStories.description: As an operator, I want revoked profiles unusable by active workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Fail lock renew with `profile_revoked` when auth profile status is `revoked`.
Workers that receive `profile_revoked` stop execution and release locks.
Cover revoke-during-run behavior with an integration test.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud submit` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a clear submit command response for automation and human use.
acceptanceCriteria: `--json` mode returns machine-readable validation error codes.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud status` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want quick visibility into run progress and health.
acceptanceCriteria: Unknown run ID exits non-zero and returns `not_found` error code.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud logs` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want historical and live run logs from terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: `logs <run-id>` returns events ordered by ascending timestamp.
Output excludes all unredacted fixture secret tokens.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud cancel` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to request cancellation from terminal with clear feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: `cancel <run-id>` sets cancel intent and returns current cancel state.
Support `--json` output with stable fields `run_id`, `cancel_requested`, and `status`.
Unknown run ID exits non-zero with `not_found` error code.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth link` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want interactive profile linking outside worker sandboxes.
acceptanceCriteria: `auth link --provider --profile` starts provider flow from the operator environment.
On success, store encrypted secret reference for the profile.
Emit an audit event containing provider and profile ID.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth import` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to import local authenticated artifacts when interactive link is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: `auth import --provider --profile --source` reads local auth artifacts from the source path.
Encrypt imported artifacts and store a secret reference.
Record an audit event with profile ID and provider.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth status` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want profile readiness visibility.
acceptanceCriteria: `auth status <profile>` shows provider, profile status, lock owner, lock expiry, and compatibility summary.
Missing profile exits non-zero with `not_found` error code.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth validate` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want explicit profile validation before submit.
acceptanceCriteria: On success update `last_validated_at`; on failure set `last_error_code`.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth revoke` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want fast profile revocation for compromised credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Emit an audit event containing profile ID and provider.
Missing profile exits non-zero with `not_found` error code.
